method: Proposes replaced token detection (RTD) as a pre-training task for text encoders.
Corrupts input by replacing some tokens with plausible alternatives sampled from a small generator network.
Trains a discriminative model (ELECTRA) to predict whether each token in the corrupted input was replaced by a generator sample or not.
Learns from all input tokens, making it more computationally efficient compared to Masked Language Modeling (MLM) which only learns from a small subset of masked tokens.
Utilizes two neural networks: a generator (typically a small masked language model) and a discriminator (ELECTRA).
The generator is trained with maximum likelihood, not adversarially, due to the difficulty of applying GANs to text.
After pre-training, the generator is discarded, and only the discriminator (the ELECTRA model) is fine-tuned on downstream tasks.
Applies this approach to pre-train Transformer text encoders.
